business_problem: SūmerSports helps NFL and college football teams make multimillion-dollar roster decisions by aggregating 14 data sources (player tracking, scouting, client data, NFL contracts) at up to 200TB per season. Its pre-Databricks stack was a fragmented patchwork of Azure-native services, open-source tools, and custom orchestration with no formal lineage or governance, forcing overly broad access patterns and making data engineering the bottleneck.
solution_summary: SūmerSports rebuilt its football-analytics stack on the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform, consolidating fragmented Azure-native services, open-source tools, and custom orchestration into a single foundation that handles ~200TB of structured and semi-structured data per NFL season from 14 sources (sensor tracking, scraped web data, in-house scouting evaluations, client data, NFL contract financials). Unity Catalog governs the data sources with curated, tagged datasets and lineage; MLflow tracks experiments and prototypes; Databricks Asset Bundles let data scientists deploy model training, cleaning, and inference jobs as infrastructure-as-code; ~95% of workloads run on serverless compute. AI/BI Dashboards, Genie, and the Databricks Assistant surface analytics to ~60 internal users and to customers. Embeddings for the SūmerBrain chatbot are prototyped on Mosaic AI and served in production through AWS Bedrock. Time-to-market dropped from 3 months to 4 weeks, and post-game insights from 4 days to under a minute.
business_outcome: SūmerSports cut time-to-market for new models from three months to four weeks (3x), accelerated post-game player insights from four days to within minutes, scaled to roughly 3x the weekly NFL data volume, and runs ~95% of workflows on serverless — letting ~50 technical and ~10 non-technical users self-serve insights from a shared governed platform.
metrics: 3x faster time-to-market for new models (3 months → 4 weeks)
Insights down from 4 days to within minutes
~3x weekly NFL season data volume vs. two years ago
architecture_clues: Single Databricks Data Intelligence Platform across ingestion, ML, and analytics
Unity Catalog over 14 curated data sources
MLflow for experiment tracking and lifecycle management
Asset Bundles as infrastructure-as-code for model deployment
Mosaic AI for embeddings and chatbot prototyping
Production model serving via AWS Bedrock
evidence_quotes: We were able to serve customers that EPA model in just under four weeks. The same work would have previously taken three months.
About 95% of our workflow is on serverless. It’s one of the biggest boons in our day-to-day operations.
In the last six months, we’ve cut it down to where it’s happening within the minute

solution_components.name: Databricks Data Intelligence Platform
solution_components.role: Single platform underpinning ~200TB/season of football-data ingestion, feature engineering, model training, and inference across ~14 data sources

solution_components.name: Unity Catalog
solution_components.role: Governs 14 primary data sources with curated, tagged datasets, lineage, and access control across product teams

solution_components.name: MLflow
solution_components.role: Experiment tracking, exploratory analysis, and lifecycle management for ML projects in a single persistent location

solution_components.name: Databricks Asset Bundles
solution_components.role: Infrastructure-as-code templates that let data scientists deploy model training, cleaning, and inference jobs to production themselves

solution_components.name: Databricks AI/BI
solution_components.role: Collaborative Notebooks and Dashboards surfacing analytics internally and to customers, with Genie enabling natural-language access

solution_components.name: Agent Bricks / Mosaic AI
solution_components.role: Prototypes the embeddings powering SūmerBrain chatbot interfaces (production models served via AWS Bedrock)

solution_components.name: Databricks Assistant
solution_components.role: Context-aware AI assistant integrated with Unity Catalog so analysts can ask routine table/availability questions without involving data engineering

solution_components.name: Databricks Serverless Compute
solution_components.role: ~95% of workflows run on serverless, eliminating cluster management for most jobs

data_flow: SūmerSports ingests ~200TB/season of structured and semi-structured football data from 14 sources (sensor telemetry, scraped web data, in-house scouting evaluations, client data, NFL contract financials) staged in AWS S3, runs nightly jobs to clean, feature-engineer, retrain, and serve inference, and stores everything under Unity Catalog as curated, tagged datasets. ML development happens in collaborative Notebooks tracked by MLflow; Asset Bundles deploy training/cleaning/inference jobs as infrastructure-as-code. AI/BI Dashboards and Genie surface analytics internally and to customers. Embeddings prototyped in Mosaic AI power the SūmerBrain chatbot, served in production through AWS Bedrock. ~95% of jobs run on serverless compute.
integration_points: 14 source systems: sensor tracking, scraped web data, in-house scouting evaluations, client data, NFL contract financials
AWS Bedrock serving production chatbot models
Team-facing analytics for coaches, scouts, and executives
Built-in monitoring integrated with SūmerSports observability tooling
